text:
There on the shelf, tucked between two forgotten volumes of children's myths, was a dusty, almost-forgotten novella, "The Whispering Woods." The protagonist, a young boy named Oliver, discovered it one crisp autumn evening while wandering in search of the mysterious whispers his late grandmother used to tell him about.

The whispers were said to come from The Whispering Woods—a place where the trees themselves were enchanted. They moved silently, murmuring secrets, dreams, and forgotten tales. Oliver was captivated by these tales, especially ones about forest sprites and mystical creatures.

After careful exploration to reassure himself that his grandmother's whispers weren't real, Oliver climbed into the woods hoping to catch whispers of her tales. As he walked deeper, the trees seemed to part around him, revealing a clearing bathed in moonlight. In the center was an old, oak tree, its trunk wide enough for him to perch.

Standing on the sturdy branches, Oliver closed his eyes. He felt the air shift
